feedback.text: Europe is going into amazing directions with consumer protection and I hope it continues this way. What I would like to focus on are the kill switches. It probably can't be helped that some software is rented out, but a lot of it, especially video games, are starting to act as if they are purchased, but rely on seller's servers to run. As soon as servers are gone, so is the software we bought. While software can be a grey area due to it's fickle nature, these kinds of practices are starting to get more common in hardware as well. Right now we're losing video games when developers decide to stop the support. Soon we might start losing phones, cars, house lights, medical devices, who knows where this might end up if we continue down this path.
